---
title: "Week 2: Sentinel-2 Sensor Overview & Applications"
author: "Haoyu Jiang"
date: "02 2026"
output: xaringan::moon_reader
---

# Sentinel-2: Multispectral Imaging for Earth Observation
- CASA0023 Learning Diary
- Author: Haoyu Jiang
- 2026.2

---

# Summary: Sensor Overview
Sentinel-2 is a high-resolution, multi-spectral imaging mission operated by ESA.It measures electromagnetic energy, not just images.
.pull-left[
**Core Specifications**  
- Twin satellites (2A & 2B) with 5-day revisit at equator  
- 13 spectral bands (VNIR → SWIR)  
- Spatial resolution: 10 / 20 / 60 m depending on band
]
.pull-right[
**Data Democracy**  
- Free, open-access imagery globally (Wulder et al., 2012)  
- Enables frequent time-series analysis for monitoring land surfaces
]

![Sentinel-2 Overview](images/Sentinel-2.jpg){width="80%"}class: inverse, center, middle

---

# Summary – Independent Thought

.pull-left[
**True scientific value**  
- Spectral band positioning > pixel size  
- **Red-edge bands (B5-B7)** capture subtle chlorophyll reflectance  

**Limitations**  
- Optical sensors → affected by cloud cover, shadows  
- Revisit frequency varies by latitude  

**ESA Level-2A products**  
- Focus on surface reflectance  
- Reduce preprocessing burden
]

.pull-right[
<img src="images/6a9e4b57ac0f45b8563262f4393c6e3804841cea-1920x1080.jpg" alt="Sentinel-2 Bands Combinations" style="width:100%; margin-bottom:5px;">
<sub style="font-size:0.3em;">Figure 1: Sentinel-2 band combinations (Visual, Bathymetric, Color Infrared, NDMI, Geology, NDVI).</sub>

<img src="images/Sentinel-2-spectral-bands-Martimort-et-al-2007.png" alt="Sentinel-2 Spectral Bands" style="width:100%; margin-top:10px;">
<sub style="font-size:0.3em;">Figure 2: Sentinel-2 spectral bands (B1–B12), highlighting Red-edge bands B5-B7, sensitive to chlorophyll content.</sub>
]

---

# Application I: Urban Radiation Budget
- Purpose: Assess urban heat islands (UHI) & impervious surface effects  
- Method: SWIR + NIR → compute **NDBI**  
$$NDBI = \frac{SWIR - NIR}{SWIR + NIR}$$
- Sentinel-2 20m SWIR differentiates reflective (cool roofs) vs heat-absorbing surfaces

<div style="display:flex; align-items:flex-start; gap:10px;">
  <img src="images/stifling-urban-heat-island-effect-captured-from-space-esa-images-2023-july-1693404931272_1280.jpg" 
       alt="Urban Heat Example" style="width:50%;">
  <div style="font-size:0.9em; line-height:1.2;">
    <strong>Figure:</strong> Satellite-derived land surface temperature (LST) map showing urban heat island effect in Rome, Italy (17 July 2023). Hot areas in red indicate high surface temperature; cooler areas in blue/green indicate lower surface temperature. Source: ESA.
  </div>
</div>
---

# Application II: Coastal Water Quality
- Purpose: Monitor eutrophication & harmful algal blooms (HABs) in urban-rural fringe water  
- Method: Coastal Aerosol band (B1) + Red-edge → detect Chlorophyll-a  
- Significance: NIR absorption vs algae reflectance allows high-frequency assessment

<div style="display:flex; align-items:flex-start; gap:12px;">
  <img src="images/skynews-algae-bloom-satellite_6075001.jpg" 
       alt="Coastal Water Example" style="width:60%;">
  <div style="font-size:0.9em; line-height:1.3;">
    <strong>Figure:</strong> High-resolution satellite image showing coastal algal blooms and water color changes due to eutrophication. Blue and green swirls indicate variations in chlorophyll-a concentration and water optical properties, suitable for monitoring coastal water quality via remote sensing.
  </div>
</div>

---
# Application III: Wildfire Burn Severity

Moving beyond routine monitoring, Sentinel-2 offers unprecedented capabilities in post-disaster ecological assessment.

.pull-left[
**Study: Burn Severity Estimation**  
- **Methodology**: Sentinel-2 red-edge spectral indices (B5–B7) to map wildfire burn severity, outperforming traditional SWIR/NIR indices (Fernández-Manso et al., 2016).  
- **Application**: Quantifying ecological damage to guide forest recovery and emergency resource allocation.  
- **Key Finding**: Red-edge indices (CIre, PSRI, NDVIre1) clearly separate four burn severity levels.
]

.pull-right[
<div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; align-items:flex-start; gap:6px;">
  <img src="images/1234.png" 
       alt="Sentinel-2 Burn Severity Scatterplot" style="width:90%;">
  <sub style="font-size:0.6em; line-height:1.2;">
    Sentinel-2 MSI spectral scatterplot matrix showing four burn severity classes:  
    Negligible to slight damage, Highly damaged, × Moderately damaged, + Destroyed.  
    Red-edge bands (B5–B7) clearly separate severity levels, enabling accurate post-fire ecological assessment (Fernández-Manso et al., 2016).
  </sub>
</div>
]
---

# Reflection: Personal Insights
- Remote sensing = physical measurement, not just imagery  
- Red-edge bands critical for vegetation and crop monitoring  
- Free, high-resolution imagery enables spatial equity research in low-income neighborhoods  


---

# Reflection: Broader Context & Future Use
- Future applications:  
  - Monitor green space degradation & heat exposure  
  - Integrate urban microclimate & retail studies  
  - Disaster risk mapping & early warning systems  
- Xaringan + Markdown + LaTeX + R → reproducible, transparent digital report  


---
